text: Find what's eating the CPU: `top` (press Shift+P to sort by %CPU) or `ps -eo pid,pcpu,comm --sort=-pcpu | head`. One process is at ~100%.
It's `report-generator`. Get its PID: `pgrep -af report-generator`. Confirm it's the offender in `top`.
Stop it: `sudo pkill -f report-generator` (or `sudo kill <pid>`; use `kill -9` only if it ignores a normal TERM). Re-check with `top` that the CPU has settled.
